votes.reason: The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking permutation equivalence, equality at odd indices, and sortedness at even indices.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification, checking permutation, equality at odd indices, and sortedness at even indices.
The Python code correctly implements the Coq specification by checking permutation via sorted lists, verifying odd indices remain unchanged, and ensuring elements at even indices are sorted.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks permutation via sorted comparison, (2) verifies odd-indexed elements are unchanged, and (3) ensures even-indexed elements are sorted in non-decreasing order. All three conditions match the Coq specification's concrete behavior.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks permutation via sorted comparison, (2) verifies odd-indexed elements are unchanged, and (3) verifies even-indexed elements are sorted in non-decreasing order. The length check is implicit in the permutation check but explicitly added. All concrete behaviors match the Coq specification.

The Python code correctly implements the Coq spec: (1) checks permutation via sorted comparison, (2) verifies odd-indexed elements are unchanged, and (3) ensures even-indexed elements are sorted in non-decreasing order. All three conditions match the Coq specification's concrete behavior.
Python checks multiset equality (permutation), preserves odd indices, and ensures the even-indexed subsequence is non-decreasing, matching the Coq spec; preconditions also align.
Python checks equal length and multiset equality, keeps odd indices unchanged, and ensures even-indexed elements in output are non-decreasing—matching the Coq spec on all inputs.

Both require no precondition; ensure output is a permutation of input; preserve all odd-index elements; and enforce non-decreasing order of elements at even indices. Python’s sorted checks and index handling match the Coq spec on all concrete inputs of integers.
